title: plantago paralias subsp. petiolata
intro: Plantago paralias subsp. petiolata, also known as sea plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain plant that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its broad, flat leaves. The medicinal properties of sea plantain include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Sea plantain is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
